steps.title: Clear denominators by multiplying by cx
steps.content:
Multiply both sides of $\frac{1}{cx} = \frac{x}{148} + \frac{1}{c}$ by $cx$:

$$1 = \frac{cx^2}{148} + x$$

Then multiply by 148 to clear the remaining fraction:

$$148 = cx^2 + 148x$$

steps.title: Rearrange into standard quadratic form
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side:

$$cx^2 + 148x - 148 = 0$$

This is a quadratic in $x$. Since the answer choices are simple integers, back-solving is the fastest approach.

steps.title: Back-solve by testing x = -37
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice $-37$:

Substitute $x = -37$ into the quadratic:

$$c(1369) + 148(-37) - 148 = 0$$
$$1369c - 5476 - 148 = 0$$
$$1369c = 5624$$
$$c = \frac{152}{37}$$

This is a valid value, confirming that the equation is satisfied when $x = -37$. Therefore, -37 is correct.
